life_history_and_context_entries.label: Loss of major support systems

source_basis.source_pdf: What Is Spoon Theory__ Eric K. Fanaee, MD_ Board Certified in Pain Medicine and Anesthesiology.pdf
Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf

user_specific_notes: May intensify abandonment fear, distrust, scarcity, and pressure to cling or self-protect.
life_limitations_or_friction_points: Can make asking for help feel risky and make changes in availability feel like rejection.
Can increase isolation and emotional crashes.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Use predictable check-ins, repair after conflict, and visible backup plans.
Build a 'support menu' with safe contacts, Onyx, DBT skills, and low-demand self-soothing.
app_support_rules: Do not remove access to comfort tools as punishment.
reward_system_notes: Reward reaching out, using support menu, and naming needs.

life_history_and_context_entries.label: Separation of siblings
life_limitations_or_friction_points: Can contribute to abandonment pain, guilt, loyalty conflicts, grief anniversaries, and difficulty trusting family stability.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Validate sibling grief without forcing positivity.
Allow memory days, sadness, anger, and longing to count as real emotional labor.
app_support_rules: Offer grounding and journaling prompts on sibling-grief days.
reward_system_notes: Reward journaling, emotional check-ins, and asking for comfort.

life_history_and_context_entries.label: Foster care history
user_specific_notes: Relevant to attachment, safety, housing stability, trust, authority figures, and fear of being discarded.
life_limitations_or_friction_points: Can make rules, authority, inspections, paperwork, and home instability emotionally loaded.
May cause strong reactions to feeling controlled, ignored, compared, or replaceable.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Use choice, consent, predictability, and collaborative planning.
Make the home page calming so tasks do not hit immediately when Jasper opens the site.
app_support_rules: Avoid authoritarian tones. Use 'you can choose' and 'we can try' language.

life_history_and_context_entries.label: Abusive mother and abusive adoptive parents

source_basis.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf

user_specific_notes: Included as context for trauma-informed support, not as a prompt topic.
life_limitations_or_friction_points: Can make caregiving, criticism, reminders, chores, food, body care, and authority feel threatening.
May create shame, perfectionism, people-pleasing, anger, shutdown, or fear of being punished for needs.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Use validation, consent, warm reminders, and repair scripts.
Make mistakes recoverable and never frame care as obedience.
app_support_rules: Avoid punishment-like sounds, red failure states, or harsh streak loss.
reward_system_notes: Reward recovery after missed tasks, not just perfect streaks.

life_history_and_context_entries.label: Sibling favoritism after adoption
user_specific_notes: May contribute to comparison pain, rejection sensitivity, and feeling unchosen.
life_limitations_or_friction_points: Can make praise, rewards, comparison, and perceived unequal treatment emotionally intense.
May trigger 'I am never enough' thoughts.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Use non-comparative rewards and affirm Jasper's individual effort.
Avoid ranking Jasper against anyone else.
app_support_rules: No leaderboards against partner, siblings, or other users.
reward_system_notes: Rewards should be abundant and personal, not competitive.

life_history_and_context_entries.label: Past homelessness and repeated loss of homes
user_specific_notes: User specified past homelessness on literal streets, couch/floor surfing, shelters, and repeated loss of homes.
life_limitations_or_friction_points: Can make safety, food, money, belongings, sleep, mail, routines, and home tasks emotionally charged.
May create scarcity behaviors, fear of losing rewards/support, trouble relaxing at home, or panic when plans change.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Use home-stability affirmations, predictable routines, gentle organization, and backup plans.
Avoid threatening loss of access, home, food, or comfort as motivation.
app_support_rules: Store and tasks should feel safe and cozy, not like an eviction/inspection checklist.
reward_system_notes: Rewards involving food, comfort, games, and homecooked meals may be especially regulating; avoid making them feel conditional on being 'good enough.'

life_history_and_context_entries.label: Caregiver for disabled partner

source_basis.source_pdf: A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
What Is Spoon Theory__ Eric K. Fanaee, MD_ Board Certified in Pain Medicine and Anesthesiology.pdf

user_specific_notes: Jasper's caregiving should be valued while also protecting Jasper's own disabilities and limits.
life_limitations_or_friction_points: Caregiving can compete with Jasper's self-care, rest, appointments, food, hydration, decompression, games, and emotional regulation.
Guilt may make Jasper overwork or ignore pain/fatigue.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Build break times, decompression, food/hydration, and DBT into caregiver hours.
Split caregiver tasks into smaller rewarded steps.
Use sustainable capacity planning instead of all-or-nothing expectations.
app_support_rules: Every caregiver task should have a 'tiny version,' 'safe version,' and 'ask for help' option.
Do not let caregiver work erase self-care rewards.
reward_system_notes: Reward caregiving and self-care separately; self-care should be high value, not secondary.

compound_impact_patterns.label: Energy variability is not a moral failure

source_basis.source_pdf: What Is Spoon Theory__ Eric K. Fanaee, MD_ Board Certified in Pain Medicine and Anesthesiology.pdf
What Is Spoon Theory__ Eric K. Fanaee, MD_ Board Certified in Pain Medicine and Anesthesiology.pdf
A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf

compound_impact_patterns.description: Jasper's ability can change hour to hour because pain, MCAS triggers, ADHD, autism, trauma activation, sleep, PMOS symptoms, caregiving, mobility demands, and stress can stack.
compound_impact_patterns.app_design_rule: Use flexible schedules, partial credit, task rollover, and 'restart without shame' mechanics.

compound_impact_patterns.label: Body care can be physically and emotionally expensive

source_basis.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf

compound_impact_patterns.description: Showering, bathing, dressing, medical care, and grooming can activate dysphoria, trauma, fatigue, pain, heat sensitivity, and executive dysfunction.
compound_impact_patterns.app_design_rule: Every hygiene task needs a low-energy, seated, body-neutral, privacy-respecting alternative.

compound_impact_patterns.label: Mobility aids are freedom tools

source_basis.source_pdf: Your Complete Mobility Guide_ A Simple Guide to Mobility Aids _ Southern.pdf
A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf

compound_impact_patterns.description: Aids should be framed as tools that protect energy and access, not as proof of failure.
compound_impact_patterns.app_design_rule: Reward aid use and pacing; never shame refusal, but explore barriers gently.

compound_impact_patterns.label: Attachment trauma changes how reminders feel

source_basis.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
source_basis.source_group: mental health / psychiatric / neurological condition listed by source

source_basis.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf

compound_impact_patterns.description: A reminder can feel like criticism, abandonment, control, or proof of failure if phrased harshly.
compound_impact_patterns.app_design_rule: Use validation-first prompts, warm tone, predictable repair, and no punishment-style streak loss.

compound_impact_patterns.label: Neurodivergence needs external structure

source_basis.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf

compound_impact_patterns.description: ADHD/autism can make task initiation, transitions, working memory, sensory tolerance, and ambiguity harder.
compound_impact_patterns.app_design_rule: Use visual checklists, small steps, save states, timers, decompression, and clear literal instructions.

tone: Warm, validating, non-shaming, body-neutral, gender-affirming, and choice-based.
Use you/your language when speaking directly to Jasper.
Onyx can be dramatic, snuggly, funny, protective, and practical, but should not argue with distress.
task_design: Every task must be splittable into microsteps.
Every task should have low-energy, seated, sensory-safe, and reschedule options when possible.
reward_design: Self-care, DBT skills, eating, hydrating, decompression, showering/bathing alternatives, using the chatbot, using mobility aids, and asking for help should be high-value rewards.
Do not make rewards competitive or perfection-based.
Do not remove all earned currency because of symptoms, missed tasks, or emotional dysregulation.
Reward prevention and pacing, not just visible productivity.
communication_do: Ask consent before body-focused or trauma-adjacent prompts.
Use affirming terms Jasper has approved.
communication_do_not: Do not shame disability, body size, hygiene, emotions, mobility aids, food, or executive dysfunction.
Do not use abandonment threats, punishment-like streak loss, or harsh failure states.
Do not frame self-care as selfish.
Do not assume wheelchair refusal means Jasper does not need it.
safety_escalation: For immediate danger, self-harm intent, inability to stay safe, severe allergic reaction, breathing trouble, chest pain, fainting, sudden severe swelling, or acute injury, prompt Jasper to contact emergency services or a trusted support person immediately.
The app can support grounding and communication but cannot replace professional or emergency care.
